volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 4.4.826c - Section 5.2.906)

sections.label: Section 4.4.826c
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter

sections.label: Section 4.4.826d
sections.translation: These wicked jades are wickedly teaching her wicked lessons .

sections.label: Section 4.4.827
sections.original: Faciés tu hanc rém mi ex paráta imparátam .
sections.translation: Instead of being all ready , they ’ ll be bringing this matter all into confusion for me . They are striving at that , wishing for it , that they may have it all undone .

sections.label: Section 4.4.829
sections.translation: Come , Olympio , as soon as you please , receive this wife of yours from us .

sections.label: Section 4.4.831
sections.original: Date érgo , datúrae si umquam éstis hodie .
sections.translation: Hand her to me then , if you are going to hand her to me at all to-day .

sections.label: Section 4.4.832
sections.translation: Be off in-doors .

sections.label: Section 4.4.832b
sections.translation: Prithee , do deal gently with her who is so young and inexperienced .

sections.label: Section 4.4.833b
sections.translation: It shall be so .

sections.label: Section 4.4.834
sections.translation: Farewell .

sections.label: Section 4.4.834b
sections.translation: Farewell ; be off now .

sections.label: Section 4.4.835
sections.translation: Is my wife now gone ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.835b
sections.translation: She ’ s in the house ; don ’ t be afraid .

sections.label: Section 4.4.835c
sections.translation: Hurra ! Now , faith , I ’ m free at last . ( Addressing himself to CHALINUS as CASINA .) My sweetheart , my spring-flower [*] , my little honey !

sections.label: Section 4.4.837b
sections.translation: But , hark you ! you ’ ll beware , if you are wise , of some mishap : she ’ s mine .

sections.label: Section 4.4.839b
sections.original: Sció , sed meus fructus est prior .
sections.translation: I know that ; but mine ’ s the first enjoyment .

sections.label: Section 4.4.840
sections.translation: Hold this torch for me .

sections.label: Section 4.4.840b
sections.translation: Why , no , I ’ ll hold her in my arms in preference . All-powerful Venus , a happy existence hast thou given me in giving me the possession of her ! A dear little body ! a dear little honey ! ( Hugs CHALINUS , who pretends to struggle .)

sections.label: Section 4.4.842b
sections.translation: O my dear little wife ! ( Jumps about on one leg .)

sections.label: Section 4.4.845
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.845b
sections.translation: She has trod upon my toes .

sections.label: Section 4.4.846b
sections.translation: I ’ ll compliment her , as it were . A mist is not so soft as is — her breast .

sections.label: Section 4.4.848
sections.original: Edepol papillam bellulam — ei misero mihi .
sections.translation: A pretty little bosom , upon my faith . ( CHALINUS gives him a thrust with his elbow , on which he roars out .) Woe to unfortunate me !

sections.label: Section 4.4.849
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.849b
sections.original: Péctus mi icit non cubito , verum ariete .
sections.translation: She struck me in the breast with her elbow .

sections.label: Section 4.4.850
sections.original: Quid tu ergo hanc , quaeso , tractas tam dura manu ?
sections.translation: Why then , pray , do you maul her about ? But she does not do so to me , who touch her gently .

sections.label: Section 4.4.852
sections.translation: O me !

sections.label: Section 4.4.852b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.852c
sections.translation: Prithee , how robust she is ! she has almost laid me flat with her elbow .

sections.label: Section 4.4.853b
sections.translation: She wishes , then , to go to bed [*] .

sections.label: Section 4.4.854
sections.translation: Nay but , why don ’ t we be off ?

sections.label: Section 4.4.854b
sections.translation: My pretty , pretty little dear !

sections.label: Section 5.1.855
sections.original: Accéptae bene ét commode éximus íntus
sections.translation: Having been well and handsomely entertained indoors , we ’ ve come out here in the street to see the wedding-sports .

sections.label: Section 5.1.857
sections.original: numquam écastor úllo dié risi adaéque ,
sections.translation: Never , upon my faith , any day did I laugh so much , nor in the time that ’ s to come do I think I shall laugh more ;

sections.label: Section 5.1.859
sections.original: Lúbet Chalinum quíd agat scire , nóvom nuptum cum nóvo marito .
sections.translation: I ’ d like to know how Chalinus gets on — the newly-married bride with her new-made husband .

sections.label: Section 5.1.860
sections.translation: and no poet ever did con trive a more artful plot than this was skilfully contrived by us .

sections.label: Section 5.1.862
sections.translation: I ’ d now very much like the old fellow to come out , with his face well battered , than whom there is not a more wicked old man alive .

sections.label: Section 5.1.864
sections.translation: Not even him do I deem to be more wicked who finds the room for him . Now , Pardalisca , do you be guard here ( pointing to the door of her house ) ; that whoever comes out from here , you may have some sport with him .

sections.label: Section 5.1.869
sections.translation: I ’ ll do it with pleasure , and in my usual way .

sections.label: Section 5.1.870
sections.original: ⋯ spectato : hinc omnia , intus quid agant ⋯
sections.translation: Observe from here everything that ’ s going on indoors .

sections.label: Section 5.1.871
sections.translation: Prithee , get behind me .

sections.label: Section 5.1.871b
sections.translation: You have liberty , too , to say freely and boldly to him anything you like .

sections.label: Section 5.1.872b
sections.translation: Be quiet ; your door makes a noise . ( They hide themselves .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.875
sections.original: Neque quó fugiam neque ubí lateam neque hoc dédecus quo modo célem
sections.translation: Neither where to fly to , nor where to conceal myself , nor how to hide this disgrace , do I know ; so much have my master and myself been supereminently disgraced at these nuptials of ours . I ’ m now so ashamed , and now so afraid , and so ridiculous are we both . But , a simpleton , I ’ m now doing what ’ s new to me : I ’ m ashamed at that which has never shamed me before . ( To the AUDIENCE .) Lend me your attention , while I repeat my exploits ; it ’ s worth your while to catch them with your ears ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.880
sections.original: ita rídicula auditu , íteratu ea súnt quae ego intus túrbavi .
sections.translation: so ridiculous to be heard , to be repeated , are these mishaps which I have met with in the house . [ When straightway [*] I had led my new-made bride into the room , I fastened the bolt ; but , however , the gloom there was just like the night . I placed , I propped things against the door ; I struggled hard [*] that before the old fellow ⋯ with my bride .

sections.label: Section 5.2.885
sections.translation: Then I began to be slow in my proceedings , for I looked behind me every now and then , lest the old fellow should break in ⋯ , a kiss , that provocative to lust , I asked of her first . She pushed back my hand , and allowed me not to give her a kiss in a quiet way .

sections.label: Section 5.2.889_890
sections.original: enim iám magis adproperó , magis iam libet ín Casinam inruere ⋯
sections.translation: But then the more anxious was I , the more desirous to assert my privilege with Casina , and I longed to do the old fellow out of that task . The door I blocked up , so that the old man might not over-power me . ( from the house .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.892
sections.translation: Come now , you accost him . ( Pointing to OLYMPIO .)

sections.label: Section 5.2.892b
sections.translation: Where is your newly-made bride ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.893_895
sections.translation: By heavens , I ’ m utterly undone ; the thing ’ s all out .

sections.label: Section 5.2.893_895b
sections.translation: It ’ s right , then , that you should relate the whole affair as it happened . What ’ s going on in-doors ? How fares Casina ? Is she quite obsequious to your will ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.897b
sections.translation: I ’ m ashamed to tell it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.898
sections.translation: Relate it in its order just as you proceeded .

sections.label: Section 5.2.899_900
sections.translation: Upon my faith , I am ashamed .

sections.label: Section 5.2.899_900b
sections.translation: Proceed boldly . After you went to bed , I want you to tell what took place after that .

sections.label: Section 5.2.902
sections.translation: But it ’ s a disgraceful matter .

sections.label: Section 5.2.902b
sections.translation: I ’ ll take care that those who hear it shall be on their guard as to mentioning it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.903
sections.translation: That ’ s the principal thing .

sections.label: Section 5.2.903b
sections.translation: You kill me with weariness . Why don ’ t you proceed ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.903c
sections.translation: — Ubi ⋯ us subtus porro ⋯

sections.label: Section 5.2.906
sections.translation: quid .
